transcript:
Watch the glowing orb drift across the black — that's the whole piece. It moves, and for a moment things flicker into existence right where it passes: ghostly letters, threads of particle motion, briefly a little tree-like form around the 28-second mark. Then it moves on and they're gone.

Here's the thing worth noticing: those 2600 particles exist everywhere on the canvas the whole time — but look at the variable holding them, "potential subjects." Outside the orb's radius they're rendered as a single almost-invisible point with barely any opacity, in a section literally commented "the void must be genuinely empty." They're not hidden behind the orb — they're barely there at all until "coherence" — a value calculated purely from distance to the thing called "address point" — pulls them into visibility.

So nothing is lurking in the dark waiting for the light. The particles don't have a secret life off-screen. They're only as real as the proximity math makes them, frame by frame. The orb isn't a flashlight revealing what exists — it's the only reason anything exists at all.
